frameworks.name: spontaneous low-frequency cortical oscillations
frameworks.one_line: ongoing ~0.1 Hz neuronal oscillations as a brain-state variable modulating evoked responses

frameworks.name: neurovascular coupling
frameworks.one_line: linking calcium transients to cerebral hemodynamics during sensory processing

frameworks.name: trial-to-trial variability of evoked responses
frameworks.one_line: framing variability as brain-state-dependent rather than noise

core_question: How do spontaneous low-frequency cortical oscillations interact with sensory-stimulus-evoked neuronal and hemodynamic responses in somatosensory cortex?
key_assumptions: ongoing low-frequency oscillations index a meaningful brain state
neuronal Ca2+ and hemodynamic signals can be coregistered simultaneously
single-trial detection is feasible without averaging given system sensitivity
manipulations: single-pulse forepaw stimulus intensity (weak vs strong)
phase/state of ongoing low-frequency oscillation at stimulus onset
key_findings: ongoing neuronal oscillations inversely modulate stimulus-evoked Ca2+ transients
high-intensity stimuli reset spontaneous oscillations to an unpreferable post-stimulus excitability
hemodynamic responses inversely interact with spontaneous hemodynamic oscillations
hemodynamic interaction correlates with the neuronal Ca2+ transient changes
interpretations: spontaneous oscillations and evoked activity compete to shape sensory cortical responses and their hemodynamic readout
